name: MEMORY.md (index)
purpose: Index file linking all canonical memories by section (User, Feedback) with one-line descriptions; not a memory itself but the root reference.
notes: No frontmatter. Treated as index, not a portable memory. Has no 'type' field. The section headers (User, Feedback) reveal the taxonomy used at time of creation. Notably missing: project and reference sections despite MEMORY.md in user-home having all four sections — canonical-memory MEMORY.md is a subset.

name: feedback_agent_teams_learnings
purpose: Operational learnings from running 4-member agent teams — tool access limits, idle floods, token cost multiplier, team sizing, and subagent-vs-team decision rule.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Agent teams operational learnings — Explore read-only, idle floods, token cost, team sizing, subagent rules

notes: Clean frontmatter. Body uses numbered list format with Why/Apply pattern. No cross-references to other memory files. The 'status: active' field is present but not part of the schema spec — worth adding to schema.

name: feedback_code_review_patterns
purpose: PR workflow and code review discipline — description quality, pre-existing rejection policy, bot sequencing, and commit discipline before pushing.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Code review and PR workflow learnings — rejection policy, description quality, bot sequencing

notes: Overlaps in content with feedback_no_preexisting_rejection.md — both contain the 'never reject as pre-existing' rule. feedback_code_review_patterns is the broader container; feedback_no_preexisting_rejection is the focused extraction. Potential supersedes relationship: no_preexisting_rejection may be a refinement of code_review_patterns on that specific rule.

name: feedback_convergence_loops_mandatory
purpose: Every significant work pass must use internal convergence loops with minimum 2 iterations — shortcuts cascade into compounding errors.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Every pass of significant work must loop internally until converged. Shortcuts cascade.

notes: Why/How-to-apply format. References a concrete prior incident (5 cascading errors) giving this rule historical grounding. Closely related to feedback_execution_failure_recovery.md — both address the 'no shortcuts, verify before acting' discipline. Together they form a pair.

name: feedback_execution_failure_recovery
purpose: Stop-diagnose-confirm discipline for failure recovery — no blind retries, no destructive shortcuts, escalate to user when blocked.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Failure recovery discipline — stop-diagnose-confirm before retrying, no destructive shortcuts

notes: Complements feedback_convergence_loops_mandatory.md — both share 'no blind retries' and 'observe cascading shortcuts' reasoning. Why/Apply format with bullet list body. No cross-references by name.

name: feedback_no_preexisting_rejection
purpose: Never auto-dismiss PR review items as pre-existing; always present the user with fix-now or track-as-tech-debt options regardless of scale.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Pre-existing is not a valid rejection reason in PR reviews — always present fix-or-track options to the user

notes: Why/How-to-apply format. Subset of feedback_code_review_patterns.md — this rule is also stated there. The focused extraction suggests this was reinforced separately and warranted its own memory. Candidate for a 'related_memories' schema field pointing to feedback_code_review_patterns.

name: feedback_parallel_agents_for_impl
purpose: Use parallel agents with convergence loops for implementation tasks rather than single-threaded work to prevent implementation theater.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Use parallel agents with convergence loops for implementation — not single-threaded work that leads to implementation theater

notes: Directly references convergence loops concept — a candidate for a 'related_memories' link to feedback_convergence_loops_mandatory.md. Why/Apply format. Introduces 'implementation theater' as a named anti-pattern. Pairs with feedback_agent_teams_learnings.md which covers team mechanics.

name: feedback_verify_not_grep
purpose: Verification must use functional end-to-end tests, not grep — grep confirms string presence, not feature functionality.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Verification must use functional tests not grep — grep confirms strings exist, not that features work

notes: IMPORTANT: The user-home MEMORY.md references this as 'feedback_grep_vs_understanding' but the canonical file is 'feedback_verify_not_grep.md'. The user-home version may be a RENAMED or SUPERSEDED copy. This is a schema divergence between canonical-memory and user-home memory. Warrants a 'superseded_by' or 'alias' field in schema. The JASON-OS MEMORY.md (canonical) correctly names 'feedback_verify_not_grep'. Why/Apply format. Cites specific prior failures as evidence.

name: session-end auto-learnings
purpose: Append-only retrospective log of data-driven insights captured at session-end across JASON-OS Foundation work, used as input for future session-begin and session-end runs.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Data-driven insights captured at session-end across the JASON-OS Foundation work. Append-only; use as input on future /session-begin and /session-end runs.

notes: Largest file at 4455 bytes. Append-only chronological log — Sessions 2 and 3 documented. Not portable (contains JASON-OS-specific session data, commit SHAs, T-ticket references). The CONCEPT is portable — SoNash would have an equivalent file specific to its session history. Frontmatter name uses spaces ('session-end auto-learnings') unlike other files which use underscore-slug names. Status field absent (no 'status: active'). This is the only project-type memory in canonical-memory.

name: user_communication_preferences
purpose: Communication style rules — concise responses, 5-8 question batches, delegation pattern, push protocol, two-locale awareness.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Communication style — concise responses, batch questions, delegation pattern, push protocol

notes: Shortest content files (469 bytes). Five bullet rules. The 'two-locale' item (work=jbell, home=jason) is user-specific but sanitized — maps cleanly to SoNash equivalent if SoNash user has a locale note. Otherwise portable as a behavioral rule template.

name: user_decision_authority
purpose: Delegation boundaries — what Claude decides autonomously vs what the user retains authority over, including the 'your call' pattern and correction protocol.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Delegation boundaries — what Claude decides vs what user retains authority over

notes: Clean four-bullet structure. Mentions CLAUDE.md specifically — 'CLAUDE.md changes' are in user-retained authority. Portable as a template; SoNash version would have same structure but possibly different specific items in the delegates/retains lists.

name: user_expertise_profile
purpose: Developer skill profile — deep Node.js/scripting/tooling expertise, frontend needs guidance, solo developer, intentionally TBD stack for JASON-OS.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Developer skill profile — Node.js/scripting/tooling expert, frontend needs guidance

notes: Contains a JASON-OS-specific note ('Stack for this project (JASON-OS) is intentionally TBD') embedded in a user-type file. This blurs the user/project boundary — the user profile is mostly portable but this line would need updating for SoNash (where stack IS known). Recommend extracting project-specific stack note to a project-type memory in SoNash.
